graphql.omission_reason: createSourceEventStream is a lower-level subscription primitive whose error contracts are fully covered by subscribe() (which calls it internally). parseValue() and parseType() are rarely called directly in app code — they are SDL-building utilities used by tooling, not runtime SaaS code. graphqlSync() and executeSync() are synchronous and have no distinct async error surface.
graphql.notes: 3 new functions contracted this pass. Key insights: (1) graphql() all-in-one function NEVER rejects — all errors (parse, validate, resolver) are captured in result.errors[]; callers that skip checking result.errors silently ignore all failures. (2) subscribe() returns a discriminated union (AsyncGenerator | ExecutionResult) — callers MUST check return type before iterating or get TypeError. (3) Non-GraphQLErrors from subscribe resolver propagate as rejections (system-level errors), not as result.errors. (4) buildSchema() throws two different error types: GraphQLError (syntax parse errors) and plain Error (semantic SDL validation errors via assertValidSDL) — instanceof checks miss semantic errors.

functions_intentionally_omitted: wallet.signMessage (async wrapper over sync crypto, no network calls)
ethers.omission_reason: Read-only GET operations (getBlock, getTransaction, getTransactionReceipt, getTransactionCount, getCode, getStorage, getNetwork, getFeeData) have no distinct error contract beyond generic SERVER_ERROR / NETWORK_ERROR already covered by getBalance. ENS utilities (resolveName, lookupAddress) have narrow adoption in SaaS backends. wallet.signMessage is an async wrapper over synchronous crypto operations with no network calls. Not counted: provider.waitForBlock() (NOT_IMPLEMENTED in source), Contract.queryTransaction() (throws @TODO, not implemented), Contract.getDeployedCode() (read-only GET), provider.getTransactionResult() (read-only GET).
ethers.notes: Depth pass 2026-04-16 (deepen-stream-2 pass 7): +3 functions contracted. Key insights: (1) BrowserProvider.getSigner() triggers eth_requestAccounts and throws ACTION_REJECTED (error.action=requestAccess) when user denies the MetaMask popup -- most common dApp wallet error; (2) Wallet.encrypt() with default scrypt N=131072 requires ~128MB RAM -- crashes Lambda at 128MB; progressCallback not awaited in serverless contexts; (3) JsonRpcProvider.getSigner() throws a plain Error (not EthersError) so isError() does not apply -- critical documentation gap. Newly discovered but skipped: provider.waitForBlock() (NOT_IMPLEMENTED), Contract.queryTransaction() (throws @TODO), Contract.getDeployedCode() (read-only), provider.getTransactionResult() (read-only). 3 scanner concerns queued (concern-20260416-ethers-deepen-5 through -7). Pass 8 (2026-04-17, deepen-stream-2): +2 functions contracted: Contract.method (contract-method-call-exception: CALL_EXCEPTION when contract reverts; contract-method-no-signer: UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION when no Signer attached), provider.send (provider-send-unsupported-method: UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION for unknown RPC methods like trace_transaction; provider-send-network-error: SERVER_ERROR/NETWORK_ERROR on connectivity failure). Coverage 67% -> 75% (18/24). 2 scanner concerns queued.

ws.omission_reason: createWebSocketStream() is a Duplex stream wrapper around an existing WebSocket — its error contracts are identical to the WebSocket error event already contracted in the WebSocket constructor entry. The stream re-emits ws errors so all error handling patterns already documented cover this function.
ws.notes: 3 new functions contracted: ping (2 postconditions: ping-before-open throws synchronously when CONNECTING; ping-callback-receives-error passes error to callback when CLOSING/CLOSED), handleUpgrade (2 postconditions: missing wsClientError handler causes silent drop of malformed requests; calling in non-noServer mode double-handles socket), WebSocketServer.close (2 postconditions: callback receives Error when server already stopped; existing connections not terminated). 3 scanner concerns queued for bc-scanner-upgrade. Key insights: (1) ping() is the most common heartbeat mechanism but throws if called before open event; (2) handleUpgrade noServer pattern is pervasive in Next.js and custom servers; (3) WebSocketServer.close() uses callback-based errors unlike most Node.js server APIs.

@libsql/client.omission_reason: sync() is an embedded replica feature only (syncUrl config), niche usage with no unique error contract. transaction.batch/executeMultiple are variants of client.batch/executeMultiple within tx context — same error profile, already covered. close() and reconnect() are synchronous and do not throw.
@libsql/client.notes: Pass 5 added 2 postconditions to execute(): execute-invalid-number-arg (RangeError when args contains NaN or Infinity) and execute-invalid-bigint-arg (RangeError when args contains bigint > 2^63-1). Both are RangeErrors thrown at argument serialization time before any network request — NOT LibsqlErrors. Confirmed from @libsql/client v0.17.2 source: sqlite3.js valueToSql() and hrana-client value.js valueToProto(). Applies to execute(), batch(), and transaction.execute(). API surface: 13 total (8 contracted, 5 omitted) — no new functions discovered. coverage_score unchanged at 0.62 (8/13) since no new function entries added; effective coverage (contracted/non-omitted) = 8/8 = 1.0.

@upstash/qstash.omission_reason: queue.get/list are read-only GETs with no meaningful error contracts beyond generic HTTP. schedules.get/list are read-only GETs. messages.get is a read-only GET. dlq.listMessages is a read-only paginated GET. events() is a deprecated alias for logs() — contractually identical. FlowControl methods (get/getGlobalParallelism/pause/resume/pin/unpin/resetRate) are advanced admin operations with narrow real-world adoption and the same HTTP error profile as other contracted methods. chat() is deprecated as of 2.7.17 and will be removed in 3.0.0.
@upstash/qstash.notes: 5 new functions contracted this pass: queue.delete (2 postconditions: api-error + data-loss for pending messages), messages.cancel (2 postconditions: api-error + result-unchecked — empty array returns {cancelled:0} not exception), dlq.delete (2 postconditions: api-error + cursor-not-checked), dlq.retry (2 postconditions: api-error + cursor-not-checked), logs (2 postconditions: api-error + cursor-not-checked). Key insights: (1) queue.delete() is IRREVERSIBLE — pending messages are silently lost without SDK warning; callers must check lag first. (2) messages.cancel() returns {cancelled:0} for empty string[] input — no throw. (3) dlq.delete() and dlq.retry() require cursor loops for complete bulk operations. (4) logs() is paginated — max 1000 per page; cursor loop required for full history. All sources confirmed from qstash-js GitHub source.

functions_intentionally_omitted: basicApi.getPage (read-only list pagination, same errors as getById)
@hubspot/api-client.omission_reason: getPage/batchApi.read are read-only operations with no distinct error contracts beyond the generic HTTP errors already covered by getById. oauth.getAccessToken is a read-only token info GET. oauth.refreshTokens archive is rarely called in SaaS app code. crm.imports is an admin-level file import API used infrequently in production SaaS request handlers.
@hubspot/api-client.notes: Both deepen-stream-1 and deepen-stream-2 worked concurrently on pass 6. Stream-1 added: batchApi.upsert (207 partial-failure silent pattern), basicApi.merge (414 merge history limit), basicApi.purge (GDPR delete, COMPLIANCE_VIOLATION label), crm.getAll (OOM risk from unbounded pagination). Stream-2 added: batchApi.create/update batch-specific postconditions (207 partial failure), timeline.eventsApi.create (OAuth-only endpoint), oauth.tokensApi.create (invalid_grant single-use codes). Critical finding: HubSpot batch operations return HTTP 207 with partial failures without throwing — callers MUST check response.errors[].

posthog-node.omission_reason: reloadFeatureFlags() swallows errors internally via .catch() — never propagates to caller (confirmed from feature-flags.js:340-345). waitForLocalEvaluationReady() returns false on timeout (not throw). captureImmediate/identifyImmediate/aliasImmediate catch errors internally with console.error — fire-and-forget from caller perspective. captureExceptionImmediate is niche error-tracking infrastructure. enable/disable are admin toggles with no unique error contract.
posthog-node.notes: 5 new functions contracted this pass. Key insights: (1) shutdown throws a STRING rejection (not Error) on timeout — 'Timeout while shutting down PostHog. Some events may not have been sent.'; (2) getRemoteConfigPayload throws synchronously (not async) if personalApiKey is missing; (3) flush() throws PostHogFetchHttpError/PostHogFetchNetworkError from @posthog/core, not generic Error; (4) reloadFeatureFlags, captureImmediate, identifyImmediate, aliasImmediate all internally swallow errors — do NOT require try-catch; (5) waitForLocalEvaluationReady returns false (not throws) on timeout. Evidence confirmed from posthog-node v5.29.2 source and @posthog/core source.

better-sqlite3.omission_reason: prepare() errors only surface at run/get/all call sites (already contracted). pragma() is read-only. function/aggregate/table are registration methods with no async error contracts. loadExtension() is niche with no standard error contract. close/serialize/defaultSafeIntegers/unsafeMode have no error contracts. Statement config methods (pluck/expand/raw/bind/columns/safeIntegers) are synchronous configuration with no throws.
better-sqlite3.notes: Deepen pass 8. 4 new functions contracted (backup, constructor, transaction, iterate) with 7 postconditions. backup-unhandled-rejection, transaction-async-function-error, iterate-deferred-execution-error are scanner-detected (ground-truth tests pass). database-file-must-exist-error and transaction-inner-exception-rollback require new scanner rules (concerns queued). Effective coverage = 8/8 non-omitted = 1.0.

functions_intentionally_omitted: add (stage files — no distinct error contract beyond GitError)
addConfig (write git config — local op, no network)
catFile/binaryCatFile (read git object — local, rarely fails)
hashObject (compute hash — local, no failure contract)
cwd (sets working dir — sync, no git call)
listRemote (list refs — network but read-only GET)
mirror (clone variant — same contract as clone)
mv (moves files — local, permission errors are generic GitError)
simple-git.omission_reason: Read-only query/list operations, local-only operations with no distinct error contracts beyond generic GitError, and variants of already-contracted methods (mirror→clone, pushTags listed separately, mv/rm local ops with no domain-specific errors beyond permission denied).
simple-git.notes: 7 new functions contracted: stash (1 postcondition: pop/apply conflicts, clean tree failure), reset (1 postcondition: HARD mode data loss, bad ref), revert (1 postcondition: conflicts leave reverting state — need abort in catch), pushTags (1 postcondition: same as push network/auth failures), deleteLocalBranch (1 postcondition: throws GitResponseError on unmerged branches — confirmed from source deleteBranchTask line 3880), submoduleAdd (1 postcondition: network call auth/URL failures), submoduleUpdate (1 postcondition: fetches submodule remotes, single failure aborts all). 7 scanner concerns queued (concerns -7 through -13). Ground-truth fixture extended with 14 new test cases (7 violation + 7 clean). TypeScript fixture compiles with no errors.

functions_intentionally_omitted: stream (errors emitted on returned stream object, same event pattern)
winston.omission_reason: stream() returns a ReadableStream that emits errors on itself — same event-listener pattern as createLogger(), already covered. child() is synchronous Object.create() with no error contract. log/write methods are fire-and-forget with no documented throws. Administrative methods (close, remove, clear, startTimer, profile, addColors) have no distinct behavioral contracts beyond synchronous return.
winston.notes: 5 new functions contracted this pass. Key insights: (1) query() uses callback pattern — err arg must be checked; (2) transports.File emits errors on the transport instance itself, NOT the parent logger — per-transport listeners required; (3) transports.Http emits failures as warn not error — the fire-and-forget HTTP log transport silently drops messages if only error listener is attached; (4) configure() throws synchronously on v2 migration options (colors/emitErrs/formatters/padLevels/rewriters/stripColors); (5) add() throws synchronously if transport lacks objectMode. exitOnError defaults to true — uncaught exceptions via handleExceptions will call process.exit(1) after 3s timeout.

tedious.omission_reason: close() is a synchronous void method with no error contract. cancel() returns a boolean synchronously. reset() is a connection pool maintenance operation rarely in hot paths — same error profile as execSql, already covered. newBulkLoad() is a factory function that creates a BulkLoad object — errors surface in the callback passed to it which is covered by execBulkLoad. connect() factory function in tedious.d.ts is a thin wrapper around Connection.connect() — same error contract, already covered.
tedious.notes: 5 new functions contracted this pass: execute (2 postconditions: parameter-validation-error, invalid-state-error), execBulkLoad (3 postconditions: constraint-violation, column-definition-mismatch, timeout), saveTransaction (2 postconditions: state-error, connection-error), transaction (2 postconditions: begin-error, commit-error), execSqlBatch (1 postcondition: invalid-state). Effective coverage: 13/13 non-omitted = 100%. API surface confirmed from tedious@18.x TypeScript declarations (lib/connection.d.ts, lib/bulk-load.d.ts, lib/tedious.d.ts). Key insights: (1) execBulkLoad is high-risk for silent data loss when constraint violations are ignored in the BulkLoad callback; (2) transaction() helper has a two-level error delivery pattern (cb(err) for begin errors, done(err) for commit errors) that is commonly mishandled; (3) execute() delivers parameter validation errors via RequestError to callback before network call; (4) saveTransaction() requires an active transaction (inTransaction=true) or throws EINVALIDSTATE; (5) execSqlBatch() has same error profile as execSql() but must be detected separately by method name.

bull.omission_reason: Read-only monitoring/status queries with no distinct error contracts beyond generic Redis errors: count methods (count, getCompletedCount, getFailedCount, etc.), state checkers (isReady, isPaused, etc.), job listing (getWaiting, getActive, getDelayed, etc.), admin reads (getJobLogs, getWorkers, getRepeatableJobs, getCountsPerPriority). Job.discard() is synchronous (not async). Job.getState() is read-only. Job.takeLock/moveToCompleted are internal queue-worker primitives.
bull.notes: Pass 4 (deepen-stream-1 2026-06-11): 3 new functions contracted. Key insights: (1) Job.moveToCompleted() — throws TypeError for non-JSON-serializable return values (circular refs, BigInt) via utils.tryCatch(JSON.stringify) + Promise.reject(); also throws same finishedErrors codes (-1/-2/-3/-6) as moveToFailed on lock/state issues; both postconditions added. (2) Job.takeLock() — returns 0/false SILENTLY when lock unavailable (no throw) — callers that skip the return value check proceed with exclusive work while another worker owns the job; critical DATA_LOSS risk identical to extendLock-zero-return pattern. Also throws ioredis Error on Redis connection failure. (3) Queue.pause() — global pause calls scripts.pause() Lua script which can fail with Redis connection errors; local pause awaits whenCurrentJobsFinished() which hangs indefinitely if a processor is stuck (no timeout built in). API surface re-enumerated against bull@4.16.5 TypeScript declarations — no new functions discovered beyond the 31 previously counted. Effective coverage now 26/26 non-omitted = 1.0.

@langchain/openai.omission_reason: getNumTokensFromMessages() uses local tiktoken library for token counting — it does NOT make API calls and does not throw API-related errors. No behavioral contract needed.
@langchain/openai.notes: Pass 1 (deepen-stream-1 pass 3, 2026-04-17): 2 new functions contracted. Key insights: (1) ChatOpenAI.stream() has TWO distinct error points — at await model.stream() (connection errors: TimeoutError, AUTH, rate-limit, ContextOverflow) AND inside for-await-of (mid-stream network failures); a try-catch around only await model.stream() does NOT catch mid-stream errors; (2) ChatOpenAI.moderateContent() should fail-safe (block content) not fail-open on API failure — rate-limit exhaustion creates a safety bypass attack vector where attackers force unsafe content through by depleting moderation quota. Error types confirmed from installed package source: wrapOpenAIClientError() in dist/utils/client.js maps APIConnectionTimeoutError -> TimeoutError, 401 -> MODEL_AUTHENTICATION, 429 -> MODEL_RATE_LIMIT, 404 -> MODEL_NOT_FOUND, context_length_exceeded -> ContextOverflowError.

functions_intentionally_omitted: listSessions (read-only, no error contract beyond filesystem)
getSessionInfo (returns undefined, no throw on miss)
getSessionMessages (read-only, returns empty array on miss)
@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk.omission_reason: listSessions/getSessionInfo/getSessionMessages/listSubagents/getSubagentMessages are read-only filesystem reads that return empty arrays or undefined when sessions are not found — they do not throw on missing sessions. No meaningful domain-specific error contracts beyond generic ENOENT filesystem errors. The pattern of returning empty/undefined rather than throwing makes them safe to call without try/catch in most contexts.
@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk.notes: Pass 5: Added unstable_v2_prompt() with 3 postconditions. Key insights: (1) unstable_v2_prompt() returns SDKResultMessage (a discriminated union) — callers MUST check result.subtype ('success' vs 'error_*') before reading result.result, otherwise get undefined silently on error variants; (2) if no result message arrives, throws Error('Session ended without result message') — requires try/catch; (3) SDKSession (V2 API) wraps a subprocess — unclosed sessions leak the subprocess; `await using` or session.close() in finally required. V2 SDKSession.send() throws Error('Cannot send to closed session') if called after close() — not contracted separately as it's covered by the session-not-closed postcondition. effective_coverage now 1.0 (9/9 non-omitted functions). 3 new scanner concerns queued (8-10) for bc-scanner-upgrade.

cross-fetch.omission_reason: response.blob/arrayBuffer/formData have the same error profile as response.text() (AbortError mid-stream, body-consumed-twice TypeError). response.buffer() is a Node.js-only non-standard method that returns a raw Buffer with the same error profile as text/arrayBuffer. response.formData() is unimplemented in node-fetch v2 and rare in SaaS hot paths.
cross-fetch.notes: Coverage score stays at 0.50 (4/8) but total function count corrected from 6 to 8 after discovering response.buffer() and response.textConverted() in node-fetch source. Key finding: textConverted() has a unique optional-dependency Error trap — the 'encoding' npm package is loaded in a try-catch at module init; if absent, textConverted() throws Error at call time with no warning at import time. 2 new postconditions: text-converted-missing-encoding-package (Error), text-converted-body-consumed-twice (same TypeError path as all other body methods).

functions_intentionally_omitted: authenticate (deprecated alias for authorizeChannel — sync, no error contract)
pusher.omission_reason: authenticate() is a deprecated synchronous alias for authorizeChannel() — both return a signature string synchronously and do not throw async errors. authorizeChannel/authenticateUser are synchronous functions, not async. createSignedQueryString and webhook are also synchronous. All 6 async-callable methods are now contracted (trigger, triggerBatch, sendToUser, terminateUserConnections, get, post). Coverage_score 0.83 because 5/6 = 0.83 (get and post are contracted; authenticate is the sync omission).
pusher.notes: 3 new functions contracted this pass: terminateUserConnections (1 postcondition: terminate-api-error — security critical in ban flows, failed termination leaves banned users connected), get (1 postcondition: get-api-error — HTTP 400 for invalid attribute on wrong channel type), post (1 postcondition: post-api-error — HTTP 413 on payloads exceeding 10KB limit). Source analysis: all three use requests.send() which throws Pusher.RequestError on status >= 400 or network errors. terminateUserConnections is particularly important as a security concern — if the Pusher API call fails during a ban operation, the user remains connected. API surface: 6 total async-callable, 5 contracted (all meaningful ones), 1 omitted (authenticate is deprecated sync).

functions_added_this_pass: config (vault error codes: vault-invalid-dotenv-key, vault-environment-not-found, vault-decryption-failed, vault-missing-data)
dotenv.omission_reason: All 5 exported functions are contracted. No intentional omissions — dotenv has a small, well-defined API surface.
dotenv.notes: 5 new postconditions added for vault mode config() errors (INVALID_DOTENV_KEY, NOT_FOUND_DOTENV_ENVIRONMENT, DECRYPTION_FAILED, MISSING_DATA). configDotenv() contracted (2 postconditions: file-not-found, partial-load-on-multi-path). decrypt() contracted (2 postconditions: invalid-key-length, wrong-key). populate() extended with OBJECT_REQUIRED throw. IMPORTANT CORRECTION: parse() prior contract incorrectly stated it throws on invalid syntax — source code shows regex-based parsing that silently skips malformed lines. New postcondition parse-silent-skip-on-malformed-lines captures actual behavior.

p-queue.omission_reason: onRateLimit() and onRateLimitCleared() are monitoring/observation methods that resolve once when the queue enters/exits rate-limiting state. No documented error cases, no failure modes beyond generic Promise rejection if queue is garbage-collected. Not in hot paths for SaaS apps.
p-queue.notes: Pass 2 (deepen-stream-2) added 1 function: onPendingZero() with 2 postconditions. Key behavioral insight: (1) onPendingZero() ignores queue.size — resolves when pending===0 even if many tasks are queued; (2) onPendingZero() resolves immediately when pending is already 0 (fast-path: if (this.#pending === 0) return). Correct use: queue.pause() then await onPendingZero() to drain in-flight before state mutation. Do NOT use as replacement for onIdle(). Effective coverage (contracted/non-omitted) = 7/7 = 1.0. 2 new scanner concerns queued (concern-20260417-p-queue-deepen-7 and -8).

ajv.omission_reason: errorsText() is a pure string-formatting utility with no documented throw behavior — takes an optional errors array and returns a human-readable string. No error contracts needed.
ajv.notes: 3 new functions contracted: compileAsync (3 postconditions: no-load-schema + load-schema-rejects + invalid-schema), addSchema (2 postconditions: duplicate-id + invalid-schema-throws), addKeyword (2 postconditions: duplicate-keyword + invalid-name). Key insights: (1) compileAsync throws synchronously when loadSchema not configured — the error escapes the promise; (2) addSchema throws on duplicate  — a common bug in server code called on every request; (3) addKeyword is permanent — keywords cannot be removed once registered, so duplicate registration from two modules crashes the process. 3 scanner concerns queued for detection rules.

@vercel/postgres.omission_reason: db is an alias for sql with identical contract — no separate entry needed. pool.sql and client.sql have identical error profiles to the already-contracted global sql tagged template. client.query has identical error profile to pool.query (already contracted). postgresConnectionString() is synchronous, returns a string or undefined, has no async behavior to contract.
@vercel/postgres.notes: DEPRECATED PACKAGE: @vercel/postgres@0.10.0 deprecated Dec 2024 — migrated to Neon. Internally delegates to @neondatabase/serverless. Key new insights: (1) VercelPostgresError is the custom error class with codes: missing_connection_string, invalid_connection_string, incorrect_tagged_template_call, invalid_connection_type. (2) createPool() throws synchronously when POSTGRES_URL missing or wrong connection type used. (3) createClient() throws synchronously on same conditions, plus requires explicit client.connect() before queries. (4) client.end() must be in finally block to prevent connection leaks — silent failures accumulate in serverless environments. (5) pool.end() must be awaited for clean shutdown. Effective coverage (contracted/non-omitted) = 8/8 = 100%.

xml2js.omission_reason: processors.* are pure synchronous string/value transformation utilities with no error contracts — they cannot throw, take no async operations, and return transformed primitives. No meaningful behavioral contract to add.
xml2js.notes: 4 functions contracted (up from 1). Key new insights: (1) parseString callback-based API commonly ignores err parameter — silent failure when XML malformed; (2) parseStringPromise resolves with null on empty string input — callers must null-check before property access; (3) Builder.buildObject() is synchronous but throws via xmlbuilder dependency for empty objects or XML-illegal characters in keys/values; (4) Parser instance parseStringPromise has same error profile as module-level function — both reject with generic Error from sax-js. 3 scanner concerns queued for callback error detection, Builder sync method detection, and null return detection.

joi.omission_reason: checkPreferences() is a low-level utility for validating preferences objects before passing to validate()/validateAsync(). It is rarely called directly by application code — typically only used in library/framework layers that build on top of joi. Its error behavior (throws AssertError on invalid prefs) is identical to compile() but with near-zero real-world usage in SaaS app code.
joi.notes: 1 new function contracted: compile() (2 postconditions: compile-invalid-schema-throws, compile-version-mismatch-throws). Key insight: compile() throws @hapi/hoek AssertError (not ValidationError) on invalid schema input — this is a DIFFERENT error type from validation failures. Callers using dynamic schema compilation (from config files, plugins, or user input) must wrap in try-catch. Static literal schema compilation is safe. 1 scanner concern queued (sync throws are currently out of scanner scope).

zod.omission_reason: safeEncodeAsync() and safeDecodeAsync() are safe variants that return result objects (success/error) rather than throwing. No meaningful error postcondition to contract — callers must check result.success but that is already covered by the safe-parse pattern. Low adoption: v4-only API, rarely used in production SaaS.
zod.notes: First depth pass. Added 2 new functions (encodeAsync, decodeAsync) and 1 new postcondition to parse() (parse-async-schema-error). Key insights: (1) parse() throws  (not ZodError) when schema has async refinements — instanceof z.ZodError checks miss this. (2) encodeAsync() throws ZodEncodeError for unidirectional .transform() schemas — already detected by scanner. (3) encodeAsync() also throws ZodError on validation failure — separate postcondition, no scanner rule yet. (4) decodeAsync() = parseAsync() in forward direction, throws ZodError. (5) Async refinements in decodeAsync() wrap external errors as ZodError issues with code: custom — dangerous for DB uniqueness checks. Effective coverage (contracted/non-omitted) = 6/6 = 100%.

@planetscale/database.omission_reason: connect() and Client.connection() are synchronous factory methods — they just construct objects and make no network calls, never throw. format() is a synchronous string sanitizer. cast() is a synchronous type-cast utility. All four have no async behavior to contract.
@planetscale/database.notes: Depth pass 2026-04-17 (deepen-stream-1 pass 2). API surface (v1.20.1): 3 async functions (execute, transaction, refresh). Added: (1) Connection.refresh() — 2 postconditions (auth failure 401/403, network TypeError); (2) UnknownError postconditions to execute() and transaction() — Cloudflare HTTP 520-530 infrastructure errors that return non-JSON produce UnknownError (extends DatabaseError), especially relevant in Cloudflare Workers deployments. Key insight: UnknownError IS-A DatabaseError so existing catch blocks handle it, but .context property with full response metadata is lost. Effective coverage: 3/3 async-callable = 100%.

image-size.omission_reason: disableTypes() and setConcurrency() are configuration-only functions: no async behavior, no throws, no error contracts. They mutate module-level globals (disabledTypes array and concurrency number). No behavioral postcondition is needed.
image-size.notes: 2 new postconditions added to imageSizeFromFile this pass: (1) empty-file — Error('Empty file') when file exists but size <= 0, confirmed from fromFile.cjs source and GitHub PR #69; (2) corrupt-image-async — TypeError when imageSizeFromFile delegates to imageSize(buffer) which throws on corrupt data; the async wrapper re-rejects with the same TypeError. Both are distinct rejection paths beyond the previously-contracted ENOENT/unsupported-format cases. 2 scanner concerns queued.

@nestjs/common.omission_reason: ParseDatePipe.transform() returns Date|null|undefined (not Promise) — synchronous. DefaultValuePipe.transform() is synchronous. Injectable/Controller/Module/etc are class decorators — synchronous, applied at class definition time. Logger methods (log, error, warn, etc.) are synchronous. HttpException and its 20+ subclasses (BadRequestException, NotFoundException, etc.) are error constructors — they are thrown by pipes but are not independently async-callable.
@nestjs/common.notes: 8 async pipe transform() functions contracted in first depth pass. Key insights: (1) ValidationPipe requires class-validator and class-transformer as peer dependencies — missing deps cause process.exit(1) on first request (cannot be caught); (2) All 6 Parse pipes (Int, Bool, UUID, Array, Float, Enum) throw BadRequestException (HTTP 400) by default via exceptionFactory; (3) ParseFilePipe throws "File is required" when fileIsRequired=true (default) and no file uploaded; (4) ParseBoolPipe is case-sensitive and only accepts "true"/"false" — "1"/"0"/"yes"/"no" all throw; (5) All pipes support exceptionFactory option to customize the thrown exception type and HTTP status. Previous contract had Injectable/Controller as sync decorators — not true async-callable functions.

react-hook-form.omission_reason: useController is a controlled component wrapper — not async, no distinct error contract beyond handleSubmit. useFormState is a read-only subscription hook — no async operations, no error contract. useWatch is a read-only value subscription — synchronous, no error contract.
react-hook-form.notes: Pass 1 (deepen-stream-2 pass 4): added 3 new function entries with 5 new postconditions. Key insights: (1) trigger() awaits async validate functions without try-catch — if validate() throws (e.g., network call), trigger() rejects; (2) trigger() returns Promise<boolean> — callers that skip await always get truthy result, bypassing validation; (3) Form component with action prop silently drops network failures and HTTP errors unless onError callback is provided; (4) useForm async defaultValues have no error callback — rejected Promise causes form to stay in isLoading state indefinitely. API surface: 9 total (6 contracted, 3 omitted read-only hooks).

simple-oauth2.omission_reason: authorizeURL() is synchronous URL builder — no async, no network calls, no throw beyond URL parse errors. createToken() is synchronous factory method. expired() is synchronous boolean check. All three have no async error contracts.

pdfjs-dist.omission_reason: Original 7 omissions unchanged (read-only GETs: getMetadata/getOutline/getDestinations/getPermissions/getFieldObjects/getAnnotations/getOperatorList). 6 new v6.x additions omitted: getStructTree (page accessibility tree, read-only), getMarkInfo (read-only), getAnnotationsByType (read-only), hasJSActions (boolean check, read-only), getCalculationOrderIds (read-only form calc), getDownloadInfo (metadata promise, read-only). All new omitted methods have no unique error contracts beyond generic worker-destroyed/HTTP errors already covered by contracted functions.
pdfjs-dist.notes: Pass 2 (2026-06-11, deepen-stream-1): 2 new functions contracted from pdfjs-dist v6.x API surface expansion. API surface expanded from 16 to 18. Key insights: (1) extractPages() (v6.x new) NEVER rejects — it resolves with null on all failures (XFA unsupported, invalid pages, serialization errors). Worker catches all errors and returns null. Callers not null-checking crash downstream. (2) getOptionalContentConfig() intent must match render() intent — mismatch throws synchronously inside render callback causing renderTask.promise rejection with Error('Must use the same intent-argument...'). (3) 6 new v6.x read-only methods omitted: getStructTree, getMarkInfo, getAnnotationsByType, hasJSActions, getCalculationOrderIds, getDownloadInfo. 3 new scanner concerns queued (concern-20260611-pdfjs-dist-deepen-1..3).

functions_intentionally_omitted: after (post-response callback, low error contract relevance)
next.omission_reason: useRouter/usePathname/useSearchParams are Client Component hooks with no async error contracts. cacheLife/cacheTag are declarative directives that configure cache behavior but do not throw meaningful behavioral errors. draftMode() is read-only with only generic HTTP errors. after() schedules callbacks post-response — no meaningful behavioral contract for SaaS usage.
next.notes: Critical behavioral insight: redirect(), notFound(), permanentRedirect() ALL throw control-flow exceptions internally. Calling them inside try-catch silently swallows them — the redirect/404 never executes. This is the #1 Next.js behavioral bug in production SaaS apps. Error codes confirmed from source: redirect() digest=NEXT_REDIRECT;..., notFound() digest=NEXT_HTTP_ERROR_FALLBACK;404. Additionally: cookies() and headers() became async in Next.js 15 — forgetting await causes silent auth failures. revalidatePath/revalidateTag must come BEFORE redirect() in Server Actions since redirect() throws. Server Actions are direct POST endpoints — always check auth.

sqlite3.omission_reason: serialize() and parallelize() are synchronous flow-control methods that don't pass errors to callbacks — they just order operations. stmt.run/get/all/each have identical error profiles to their Database equivalents (syntax-error, constraint-violation, database-locked) and share the same contract coverage. wait() and interrupt() are synchronous utilities with no async error contracts.
sqlite3.notes: 4 new functions contracted this pass: each (2 postconditions: each-completion-error-ignored + each-syntax-error — the dual-callback pattern where completion callback frequently omitted), close (2 postconditions: close-unfinalised-statements SQLITE_BUSY + close-already-closed SQLITE_MISUSE), loadExtension (2 postconditions: file-not-found + security-disabled), backup (2 postconditions: backup-cantopen-destination + backup-misuse-after-finish). Key insights: (1) db.each() has the most dangerous silent failure pattern — if completion callback is omitted, ANY SQL error is silently swallowed and the app proceeds with partial results. (2) db.close() fails with SQLITE_BUSY if any stmt.prepare() result is not finalized — confirmed from test/open_close.test.js. (3) backup() BUSY/LOCKED errors are retried automatically via retryErrors=[BUSY,LOCKED] but CANTOPEN is not retried. (4) loadExtension() is disabled test in the test suite — confirmed error passes to callback.

mammoth.omission_reason: readEmbeddedStyleMap is read-only (returns null when no style map is embedded, no destructive side effects), not present in the TypeScript declarations (index.d.ts), and has the same file/zip error profile as the already-contracted functions. unzipper.notes: 6 new functions contracted this pass: ParseOne (2 postconditions: PATTERN_NOT_FOUND + corrupt-archive), Open.buffer (1 postcondition: FILE_ENDED for invalid buffer), Open.url (2 postconditions: 'Missing content length header' + network error), Open.s3_v3 (1 postcondition: NoSuchKey/AccessDenied), CentralDirectory.extract (2 postconditions: PATH_MISSING + filesystem errors), Entry.buffer/File.buffer (2 postconditions: MISSING_PASSWORD + BAD_PASSWORD + decompression errors). (2) cast() throws TypeError when assert:true (default) and cast result fails type check; confirmed from index.js line 772. (3) validateSync() throws plain Error (not ValidationError) when it encounters async test() functions; confirmed from index.js line 386. API surface: 7 total (5 contracted, 2 omitted). validator.notes: 8 new functions contracted this pass. axios-retry.notes: 4 new behavioral contract functions added (5 new postconditions). stripe-v21.omission_reason: oauth.authorizeUrl() is synchronous and returns a URL string. It has no async behavior, no network calls, and no error contract worth enforcing. All other v21-specific functions are async or throw synchronously.
stripe-v21.notes: Pass 1 (2026-06-11): 3 new function contracts added for stripe-node v21 thin event API. Key insights: (1) parseEventNotification() is the v2 equivalent of constructEvent — throws StripeSignatureVerificationError + new plain Error guard for wrong payload type (v1 payload passed to v2 method). (2) fetchRelatedObject() returns Promise<null> (not throws) when no related_object — callers must null-check, this is a silent failure path. (3) Both fetchRelatedObject() and fetchEvent() can throw TemporarySessionExpiredError (NEW v21 error class, rawType: "temporary_session_expired") when StripeContext session expires — NOT caught by StripeRateLimitError handlers. (4) RateLimitError in v2 paths is a NEW class distinct from StripeRateLimitError. Coverage: 4/7 functions (57%) → 6/7 (86%) raw; effective 6/6 non-omitted = 100%.
